findings.detail: KEYWORD_FAMILIES are hardcoded domain keyword lists (semiconductors, gold, crypto, etc.) — no external injection. detect_keyword_triggers() does case-insensitive substring matching on user_text.lower(). At most one domain trigger per session (self._triggered_domains dedup). No regex injection — keywords are plain strings.
Event IDs generated via hashlib.sha256 with structured input (event_type + headline + date). First 16 hex chars used. No user-controlled content constructable to cause collisions maliciously.
All regex patterns are hardcoded string literals (cb_keywords, geo_keywords, personnel_keywords). No user-supplied regex patterns. re.search with re.IGNORECASE on headline strings — safe.
No LLM calls, no network I/O, no file I/O. Pure text processing on pre-fetched news_items. vol_shock detection operates on dict parameter (not externally sourced in this module).
datetime.now(timezone.utc) used for event detection timestamps and vol_shock date hashing. No bare datetime.now() calls.

This module is a generic event detector — no beta shadow-specific code. Keyword triggers are domain-based, not shadow-type-based. Isolation is enforced downstream in shadow_mother.py which controls who receives triggers.
